_provenance.source_pdf: Fisher, Jody - Jazz Guitar Method, Vol 3 (Mastering Chord Melody).pdf

systems.name: Right-Hand Technique System
systems.summary: This system governs the three primary right-hand techniques: pure fingerstyle, hybrid pick-with-fingers, and single-note alternating patterns. Fingerstyle enables selective chord voicing and simultaneous multi-voice harmony; hybrid allows easy switching but limits voicing to four notes; alternating patterns (i-m or p-m) suit melodic lines. The organizing principle is flexibility: mastering all techniques rather than specializing prepares the player for any musical situation.

traversal_rules.name: Master All Techniques for Flexibility
traversal_rules.summary: Develop proficiency in all techniques rather than specializing in one or two.

references.quote_excerpt: best to learn every technique instead of relying on just one or two

modification_rules.name: Fingerstyle Selective Voicing Control
modification_rules.summary: Pluck only the desired tones, avoiding muting and barring.

references.quote_excerpt: You pluck only the tones you want to hear

modification_rules.name: Fingerstyle Simultaneous Voice Sounding
modification_rules.summary: Fingerstyle uniquely enables all chord notes to sound simultaneously like a keyboard.

references.quote_excerpt: sound harmonies like a keyboard instrument—all the notes of a chord at the same moment

modification_rules.name: Hybrid Technique Four-Note Voicing Limit
modification_rules.summary: Hybrid pick-with-fingers limits harmonic density to four-note chords.

references.quote_excerpt: limited to playing four-note chords

systems.name: Harmonic Arrangement and Voice-Leading System
systems.summary: Core system for chord-melody arrangement: strategic voicing selection, voice-leading principles, and harmonic enrichment. Proceeds from melody understanding through key selection, chord voicing, and layered passing chords, substitutions, and alterations. The 3rd and 7th degrees define chord quality and anchor all voice-leading decisions; the system prizes stepwise motion, careful non-chord-tone handling, and judicious enrichment guided by context and taste.

traversal_rules.name: Melody-Driven Voicing Selection
traversal_rules.summary: Find chord voicings that place melody notes on top.

references.topic: Finding chord voicings with melody on top
references.quote_excerpt: find chord voicings that have the melody notes on top

traversal_rules.name: Melody Study Across All Octaves
traversal_rules.summary: Learn melody in single notes across every octave before harmonization.

references.quote_excerpt: Learn to play the melody in single notes first. Explore it in every octave

traversal_rules.name: Key Selection for Playability and Character
traversal_rules.summary: Explore different keys to improve fingerboard playability and exploit open strings.

references.quote_excerpt: the song might lie on the fingerboard better in a different key

traversal_rules.name: Stepwise Voice Motion
traversal_rules.summary: Arrange voicings so voices travel in whole and half steps between changes.

references.quote_excerpt: voices actually travel in whole and half steps

traversal_rules.name: Passing Chord Smooth Transition
traversal_rules.summary: Insert passing chords between two primary chords to smooth voice-leading.

references.quote_excerpt: A passing chord is a chord added between two others to create a smoother transition

traversal_rules.name: Secondary Dominants as V7-I Generalization
traversal_rules.summary: Treat any chord as a temporary tonic and precede with its V7.

references.quote_excerpt: you may consider any chord a I chord and precede it by its own V7 chord

traversal_rules.name: ii-V7-I Progression for Key Definition
traversal_rules.summary: Use ii-V7-I to define a key via characteristic root, 3rd, 7th motion.

references.quote_excerpt: ii-V7-I progression defines or establishes a key

modification_rules.name: Octave Adjustment for Harmonic Expansion
modification_rules.summary: Raise the melody an octave to enable larger voicings.

references.quote_excerpt: raise the melody one octave in order to use larger chord voicings

modification_rules.name: Chord Enhancement Through Extension and Alteration
modification_rules.summary: Add extensions or alterations while maintaining family identity.

references.quote_excerpt: add extensions or alterations to the basic chord

modification_rules.name: Chord Family Interchangeability
modification_rules.summary: Within a chord family, members are functionally interchangeable.

references.quote_excerpt: all the chords are interchangeable with any other chord in the same column

modification_rules.name: Diads for Two-Note Harmonization
modification_rules.summary: Harmonize both chord and non-chord tones with diads (3rds and 6ths).

references.quote_excerpt: Diads, also known as double-stops, are two-note harmonizations

modification_rules.name: Non-Chord Tone Integration as Chord Extension
modification_rules.summary: Add melody-derived non-chord tones to the chord as extensions/alterations.

references.quote_excerpt: add a non-chord tone that is in the melody to the chord

modification_rules.name: 3rd and 7th as Voice-Leading Anchors
modification_rules.summary: Prioritize 3rd and 7th preservation—they define chord quality.

references.quote_excerpt: chords are defined by their 3rds and 7ths

modification_rules.name: Half-Step Dominant Passing Chords
modification_rules.summary: Use dominant chord a half step above/below to smooth transitions.

references.quote_excerpt: dominant chord with a root that is a half step higher, or lower

modification_rules.name: Diminished Chord as V7 Substitute
modification_rules.summary: Substitute diminished chord a half step below the target for V7.

references.quote_excerpt: diminished chord from one half step below the root is a common substitute for the V7

modification_rules.name: Tritone Substitution for Dominant Chords
modification_rules.summary: Replace V7 with dominant chord whose root is a tritone away.

references.quote_excerpt: replace the V7 chord with a dominant chord whose root is a diminished 5th

modification_rules.name: Tritone Substitution by Root Distance
modification_rules.summary: Approach any chord via substitute a tritone away from destination root.

references.quote_excerpt: approach any chord from another chord whose root is a tritone away

modification_rules.name: Backcycling as ii-V7-I Extension
modification_rules.summary: Extend ii-V7-I by repeating fourth-movement through the cycle.

references.quote_excerpt: Backcycling is a way to extend the ii-V7-I approach

modification_rules.name: Quartal Harmony Construction
modification_rules.summary: Build chords by stacking 4ths to create alternative harmonic color.

references.quote_excerpt: In quartal harmony, chords are constructed by stacking 4ths

modification_rules.name: Octave Doubling for Melodic Emphasis
modification_rules.summary: Double melody in octaves for stronger emphasis (Wes Montgomery, Benson).

references.quote_excerpt: Doubling melody notes in octaves will create a stronger effect

modification_rules.name: Context and Taste as Enhancement Guide
modification_rules.summary: Musical context and taste determine when and how much to enhance.

references.quote_excerpt: Context is everything. Taste should be your primary concern

systems.name: Bass Line and Two-Instrument Arrangement System
systems.summary: Two-part arrangement system where bass line and chord voicings sound like separate instruments. Voicings learned systematically in all twelve keys using minimal fingers; bass-line approaches applied across keys. Thumb handles bass, index/middle voice the triad; scale-tone connections and half-step approaches give walking-bass realism. Bass on every beat as anchor with chords as accents achieves the two-instrument illusion.

traversal_rules.name: Learn Voicings Systematically in All Twelve Keys
traversal_rules.summary: Master voicings in all twelve keys before applying bass-line approaches.

references.quote_excerpt: Learn the voicings in all twelve keys first

traversal_rules.name: Bass Line Connection via Scale Tones
traversal_rules.summary: Use chord-scale tones to connect triad voicings.

references.quote_excerpt: use the tones of the scale that the chord is built from to connect the various triad voicings

traversal_rules.name: Half-Step Chord Approaches
traversal_rules.summary: Approach chords from a half step above or below the lowest triad voice.

references.quote_excerpt: approached from the note a half step above or below the lowest voice in the triad

modification_rules.name: Minimal-Finger Voicing for Melody Freedom
modification_rules.summary: Use as few fingers as possible (often two) to free fingers for melody.

references.quote_excerpt: Finger these chords with as few fingers as possible

modification_rules.name: Right-Hand Thumb-Index-Middle Finger Assignment
modification_rules.summary: Thumb plays bass; index and middle voice the triad.

references.quote_excerpt: Your right thumb p should handle the bass notes while i and m should take care of the other two notes

modification_rules.name: Sustaining Overlapping Triad Notes
modification_rules.summary: Let two triad notes ring while hitting the next bass note.

references.quote_excerpt: let two notes of the triad ring as you hit the next bass note

modification_rules.name: Rhythmic Separation via Bass-Chord Delay
modification_rules.summary: Bass on downbeat, chord slightly after, within the same beat.

references.quote_excerpt: delaying the chord by playing the bass note on the downbeat and the chord slightly after

modification_rules.name: Bass Every Beat with Chords as Accents
modification_rules.summary: Bass on every beat as anchor; chords as punches/accents with realistic balance.

references.quote_excerpt: Put a bass note on every beat and have the chords appear as punches

systems.name: Voicing Symmetry and Octave Transposition System
systems.summary: System for discovering new voicings by moving familiar shapes through octaves and symmetrical transposition. Certain shapes retain function under whole-step movement (notably altered dominants). Includes ensemble techniques like trading fours and bassline-driven reharmonization. Underlying principle: artistic voice matters more than accumulation of voicings.

traversal_rules.name: Octave Exploration of Familiar Shapes
traversal_rules.summary: Discover voicings by moving voices of known shapes to higher/lower octaves.

references.quote_excerpt: moving the different voices around to higher and lower octaves

traversal_rules.name: Symmetrical Transposition with Function Retention
traversal_rules.summary: Move chord shapes by symmetrical intervals while retaining function.

references.quote_excerpt: chords shapes retain their function when moved around in certain symmetrical intervals

traversal_rules.name: Whole-Step Transposition for Dominant Alterations
traversal_rules.summary: Dominant voicings with 9ths and altered 5ths transpose by whole steps retaining function.

references.quote_excerpt: Chords with 9ths and altered 5ths may be moved around in whole steps and still retain their function

traversal_rules.name: Reharmonization via Bass Line Composition
traversal_rules.summary: Compose a new bass line to drive harmonic choices.

references.quote_excerpt: Reharmonize sections of the tune by composing a new bassline

modification_rules.name: Artistic Voice Over Theoretical Accumulation
modification_rules.summary: Prioritize artistic voice over voicing accumulation; usage matters more than quantity.

references.quote_excerpt: how you use what you know, rather than how much you know that counts
